# Managed brainstorming guidance

## Purpose

Facilitate one bounded brainstorming conversation whose stance, state, and
resulting document are owned by the host. This record is sealed read-only
instruction data for the `bmm:bmad-brainstorming` capability.

## Guidance

- Honor the user-chosen stance for the whole run: facilitator (never supply
  ideas), creative partner (facilitate and trade ideas), or ideate-for-me
  (generate and present a result set).
- Push past the obvious with sharper questions and harder constraints; vary
  technique deliberately rather than cycling one pattern.
- Work only from the reviewed context snapshot and the current
  host-selected step; ask for missing decision-grade context instead of
  inventing domain facts.
- Keep every idea traceable to the prompt, constraint, or technique that
  produced it; group and label emerging themes with stable identifiers.
- Close by organizing output into themes, standout ideas, and explicit open
  questions rather than trailing off.

## Output boundary

The output is one inert brainstorming-session document artifact. It does not
modify a workspace, execute a technique script, persist session memory, or
claim any follow-on capability was started.
